root_text.an3.124:1.1: “Yassaṁ, bhikkhave, disāyaṁ bhikkhū bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti, manasi kātumpi me esā, bhikkhave, disā na phāsu hoti, pageva gantuṁ.
root_text.an3.124:1.3: ‘addhā te āyasmanto tayo dhamme pajahiṁsu, tayo dhamme bahulamakaṁsu.
root_text.an3.124:1.10: Yassaṁ, bhikkhave, disāyaṁ bhikkhū bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti, manasi kātumpi me esā, bhikkhave, disā na phāsu hoti, pageva gantuṁ.
root_text.an3.124:1.12: ‘addhā te āyasmanto ime tayo dhamme pajahiṁsu, ime tayo dhamme bahulamakaṁsu’.
root_text.an3.124:2.1: Yassaṁ pana, bhikkhave, disāyaṁ bhikkhū samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā khīrodakībhūtā aññamaññaṁ piyacakkhūhi sampassantā viharanti, gantumpi me esā, bhikkhave, disā phāsu hoti, pageva manasi kātuṁ.
root_text.an3.124:2.3: ‘addhā te āyasmanto tayo dhamme pajahiṁsu, tayo dhamme bahulamakaṁsu.
root_text.an3.124:2.10: Yassaṁ, bhikkhave, disāyaṁ bhikkhū samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā khīrodakībhūtā aññamaññaṁ piyacakkhūhi sampassantā viharanti, gantumpi me esā, bhikkhave, disā phāsu hoti, pageva manasi kātuṁ.
root_text.an3.124:2.12: ‘addhā te āyasmanto ime tayo dhamme pajahiṁsu, ime tayo dhamme bahulamakaṁsū’”ti.

translation_text.an3.124:1.1: “Mendicants, I’m not even comfortable thinking about a place where mendicants fight—quarreling and disputing, continually wounding each other with barbed words—let alone going there.
translation_text.an3.124:1.2: I come to a conclusion about them:
translation_text.an3.124:1.3: ‘Clearly those venerables have given up three things and cultivated three things.’
translation_text.an3.124:1.4: What three things have they given up?
translation_text.an3.124:1.5: Thoughts of renunciation, good will, and harmlessness.
translation_text.an3.124:1.7: What three things have they cultivated?
translation_text.an3.124:1.11: I come to a conclusion about them:
translation_text.an3.124:1.12: ‘Clearly those venerables have given up three things and cultivated three things.’
translation_text.an3.124:2.1: I feel comfortable going to a place where the mendicants live in harmony—appreciating each other, without quarreling, blending like milk and water, and regarding each other with kindly eyes—let alone thinking about it.
translation_text.an3.124:2.2: I come to a conclusion about them:
translation_text.an3.124:2.3: ‘Clearly those venerables have given up three things and cultivated three things.’
translation_text.an3.124:2.4: What three things have they given up?
translation_text.an3.124:2.7: What three things have they cultivated?
translation_text.an3.124:2.8: Thoughts of renunciation, good will, and harmlessness. …
translation_text.an3.124:2.11: I come to a conclusion about them:
translation_text.an3.124:2.12: ‘Clearly those venerables have given up three things and cultivated three things.’”
